# Dirham — UAE Dirham Currency Symbol

> Interactive demo and documentation for the `dirham` npm package — UAE Dirham (د.إ) currency symbol as a web font, CSS, and React component.

## About This Site

This is the live demo and documentation site for the `dirham` npm package. It showcases:
- The Dirham symbol (U+20C3) rendered with a custom web font
- React SVG component (`DirhamSymbol`) with 9 weight variants
- Font icon component (`DirhamIcon`) using CSS @font-face
- JavaScript formatting utilities (`formatDirham`, `parseDirham`)
- Font pairing previews with popular typefaces (Geist, Inter, Roboto, Cairo, Vazirmatn, etc.)
- 5 font variants: default, sans, serif, mono, and Arabic

## Links

- This site: https://pooya.blog/dirham/
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/dirham
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/pooyagolchian/dirham
- Package llms.txt: https://pooya.blog/dirham/llms-package.txt

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install dirham
```

### React (Recommended)
```tsx
import { DirhamSymbol } from "dirham/react";
<DirhamSymbol size={16} weight="regular" />
```

### CSS Web Font
```ts
import "dirham/css";
```
```html
<i class="dirham-symbol" aria-label="UAE Dirham"></i>
```

### JavaScript
```ts
import { formatDirham, parseDirham, DIRHAM_UNICODE } from "dirham";
formatDirham(1234.5); // "د.إ 1,234.50"
```

## Unicode

The UAE Dirham sign is at codepoint U+20C3, accepted for Unicode 18.0 (expected September 2026). This package maps the glyph to U+20C3 via a custom web font. When OS fonts adopt Unicode 18.0, the web font gracefully becomes unnecessary with zero migration.

## License

MIT — Glyph sourced from the Central Bank of UAE.
